title: Dog Rabies Vaccination Laws by State (2026)
note: Compiled from the Animal Legal & Historical Center (Michigan State University) rabies-law table and the NASPHV Compendium of Animal Rabies Prevention. State law is a floor: counties and cities frequently impose stricter or more frequent requirements, and licensing rules differ. Confirm with your local animal-control authority and veterinarian. Titer tests are not accepted in lieu of vaccination in any state.

rows.detail: State law mandates rabies vaccination for dogs. Annual boosters are required regardless of the vaccine's labeled duration.
State law mandates rabies vaccination for dogs. First dose is valid 1 year, then boosters follow a 3-year cycle if a 3-year vaccine is used.

rows.interval: Quarantine on entry (no rabies in wild)
rows.detail: Hawaii is the only rabies-free U.S. state; instead of a vaccine mandate it enforces strict import quarantine and testing.
